data.current_summary: Get the list of all the events for the received emails.
data.current_description: This endpoint will show the list of all the events for the received emails.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all inbound email events with filtering
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all events associated with received emails. Supports filtering by email status, date range, and other criteria through query parameters. Returns an array of event objects containing event type, timestamp, email metadata, and status information. Useful for tracking email delivery, bounce, complaint, and engagement events.
data.reasoning: Original description is repetitive and lacks specificity. Added information about pagination support, filtering capabilities, return value structure, and use cases. This helps AI agents understand what parameters are available and what data will be returned.

message: Operation 'get-inbound-email-events-by-uuid' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is repetitive and lacks detail about return value structure and use cases. Enhanced description clarifies the chronological ordering, types of events returned, and practical applications for AI agents.

data.current_summary: Fetch all events history for one particular received email.
data.current_description: This endpoint will show the list of all events history for one particular received email.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete event history for a specific received email identified by its UUID. Returns a chronologically ordered list of all events associated with that email, including delivery status, processing events, and any errors encountered. Useful for debugging email delivery issues or auditing email processing.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is repetitive and lacks detail about return value structure and use cases. Enhanced description clarifies the chronological ordering, types of events returned, and practical applications for AI agents.

message: Operation 'get-inbound-email-attachment' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is adequate but description is minimal. Enhanced description clarifies how to obtain the download token, what is returned (file with headers), authentication requirements, and token validity constraints. This helps AI agents understand the complete workflow and prerequisites for using this operation.

data.current_summary: Retrieve inbound attachment with download token.
data.current_description: This endpoint will retrieve inbound attachment with download token.
data.description_suggestion: Downloads an inbound email attachment using a download token. The token is typically obtained from inbound email event data. Returns the attachment file with appropriate content-type headers. Supports single attachment retrieval per request. Requires valid authentication and a valid, non-expired download token.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description is minimal. Enhanced description clarifies how to obtain the download token, what is returned (file with headers), authentication requirements, and token validity constraints. This helps AI agents understand the complete workflow and prerequisites for using this operation.

message: Operation 'get-email-event-report' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary mentions 'activity' which is vague. data.current_summary: Get all your transactional email activity (unaggregated events)
data.current_description: This endpoint will show the aggregated stats for past 30 days by default if `startDate` and `endDate` OR `days` is not passed. The date range can not exceed 90 days
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves individual transactional email events (unaggregated) for analysis and monitoring. Returns events from the past 30 days by default. Supports custom date ranges via `startDate` and `endDate` parameters or `days` parameter, with a maximum range of 90 days. Useful for detailed event-level tracking and troubleshooting.
data.reasoning: Original summary mentions 'activity' which is vague. Description contradicts itself by saying 'aggregated stats' when the endpoint returns unaggregated events. Missing clarity on what data is returned and the distinction between this and the aggregated report endpoint. Enhanced version clarifies the unaggregated nature, default behavior, parameter options, and use cases for AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Get your transactional email activity aggregated over a period of time
data.current_description: This endpoint will show the aggregated stats for past 90 days by default if `startDate` and `endDate` OR `days` is not passed. The date range can not exceed 90 days
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves aggregated statistics for transactional email activity over a specified period. Returns summary metrics for the past 90 days by default. Supports custom date ranges via `startDate` and `endDate` parameters or `days` parameter, with a maximum range of 90 days. Aggregated data provides high-level insights into email performance and delivery metrics.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specificity about what metrics are returned and the distinction from unaggregated events. Enhanced version clarifies the aggregated nature of data, default behavior, parameter options, and use case for performance analysis.

data.current_summary: Get the personalized content of a sent transactional email
data.current_description:
<Note title="How to get uuid?">
You can get the uuid using either of the following methods:

Send a GET request to https://api.brevo.com/v3/smtp/emails and pass the message_id in the url. Use your api-key to authenticate the request and you will get your uuid as a response.

The uuid can also be fetched from the transactional logs page in your Brevo account, from the address URL.
</Note>
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the full personalized content of a previously sent transactional email, including rendered HTML, plain text, and recipient-specific variables. Requires the email UUID (obtainable via the GET /smtp/emails endpoint using message_id or from the Brevo account transactional logs). Returns the complete email content as it was delivered to the recipient, including subject, body, and any personalized data.
data.reasoning: Current description focuses on how to obtain UUID rather than what the operation does and returns. Reorganized to clarify the operation's purpose, required input (UUID), how to obtain it, and what is returned (full email content with personalization).
